metrics.gates_first_pass_note: Biome import sort + formatting in 7 files (auto-fixed). YAML parse error in post-incident.yaml (\| escape sequences) caught by e2e, fixed manually.

hypotheses.name: 3-phase sprint works across project types
hypotheses.evidence: 6th sprint completed cleanly: THINK (Gherkin + wave plan), EXECUTE (4 waves, 6 subagents), SHIP (metrics + retro). Process is now well-exercised across feature, integration, and persistence sprints.

hypotheses.name: Wave parallelism helps
hypotheses.evidence: 28% delegation ratio. Wave 1 ran 2 parallel haiku+sonnet agents (post-incident workflow + export module). Wave 2 ran 1 sonnet (custom loader). Wave 3 ran 1 sonnet (UI integration). Clear task boundaries enabled effective parallelism.

hypotheses.name: Gates catch real issues
hypotheses.evidence: Biome caught import ordering and formatting in 7 files. E2E tests caught YAML parse failure in post-incident.yaml (\| escape sequences invalid in YAML double-quoted strings). Without e2e gate, the broken workflow would have shipped.

hypotheses.name: Skills are followed by agents
hypotheses.evidence: 4/5 H7 audit instructions followed. Security auditor instruction (file validation) passed — custom-loader validates file size, extension, and content. UX instruction (error messages) passed — clear, actionable errors. PM instruction (end-to-end testable) passed. Architect instruction (typed errors) passed. PE instruction (test isolation) partially — subagent-generated tests needed biome formatting fixes, indicating imperfect adherence to project conventions.

hypotheses.name: Haiku is viable for mechanical tasks
hypotheses.evidence: Haiku created post-incident.md (correct format) but post-incident.yaml had invalid YAML escapes (\|). Haiku also ran codebase exploration successfully. Mechanical content generation works well; YAML syntax awareness is a gap.
